Bill Clinton joins this week's podcast! | Derek, the fifth episode of our podcast “With her” is here, and host Max Linsky is chatting with a **[special guest you definitely don’t want to miss:](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjY2NzYwODgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTAwMjU1NjQwS0&mt=1&rt=0)** [

As you’d expect from President Clinton, the conversation touches on a little bit of everything -- from Monday night’s debate, to how Hillary is different now than she was 40 years ago, to her not-so-secret love of reality TV.

You’ll also learn about the world leaders who inspired him, hear how a favorite Hemingway quote reveals something important about Hillary, and learn the significance of a 3.6 billion-year-old rock he used to keep in the Oval Office (hint: It’s out of this world!).

body:
Claim your beautiful, limited-edition print of the greatest president in living memory. |

Friend, We just finalized the details of an incredible opportunity for my best supporters, and I couldn’t wait to let you know about it. Renowned artist Steve Penley – often referred to as “The Republican Party’s Favorite Artist” – has agreed to produce a limited number of prints of one of his iconic Ronald Reagan portraits. These beautiful, special edition Reagan prints are available EXCLUSIVELY to outstanding supporters of my campaign. body:
Add your name to the list of loyal Trump supporters in your area.|

Friend, My father wants a list of all the truly loyal Trump supporters in your zip code printed out for him. That’s why I am emailing you today, Friend. You stand out among our most active supporters in your zip code and probably anywhere in America. So when I print out a list of supporters from this week to give to my father on Tuesday night, I think your name deserves to be printed at the top, Friend. I hope you’ll make a donation right now to make sure that happens. With Hillary Clinton rolling in money from Wall Street, Hollywood, and the DC elites that have rigged your own government against you, my father is proud of men and women like you who have joined his movement to take back our country. I have watched first-hand as my father has negotiated incredible deals to turn things around and make things better for workers and investors. I have watched him put his vision, energy and experience to work and score tremendous achievements when others could not see the possibilities at all. That is the kind of attitude we need in the White House. And that is why my father needs to know you are standing with him, Friend. Just chip in $35 or more, and your name will be on the list I print out and hand to my father Tuesday night. Thanks. Eric Trump ADD YOUR NAME TO MR. TRUMP’S LIST OF LOYAL SUPPORTERS

Conservatism is a fight for principles, policies, and ideas. We win the battle of ideas. And if you’re committed to a BIG Republican victory on November 8, then I need you to complete our BIG IDEAS SURVEY to help us win just 36 days from now. name: Maggie Hassan
subject: What will decide this race
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello there,
It's been almost a year since I got in this race to fight for Granite State families in the U.S. Senate. It's hard to believe, but now there's just over a month left until this election is over.
In the last year we've seen Senator Kelly Ayotte's corporate special interest backers throw everything they can at us, like $47 million in misleading ads. They know I will always put our families before their agenda, and frankly, they're scared. They also know that the Senate majority is on the line and that our race is their greatest chance at holding on to it.
We're fighting back as hard as we can, but we need you to join us. **[Please, give $3 or whatever you can today so that we can fight these attacks, win this election, and get the Senate working for hard-working families.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd978/2a380d31/16a49a3d/fd3ba9/2309204348/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYxMDAzX01XSF9uaC1uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20161003_MWH_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")**
Virtually every poll we've seen has had us within the margin of error, and the pundits are calling this race a toss-up. That means that this race is going to be decided by which team can work harder and turn out more people. Simply put, you can help determine this race.
**Every dollar you give will help us reach voters and turn out your neighbors.**
I've talked with our team, and they think you might be able to give $3 today. [**I'm coming to you directly -- please give today so that we can win in New Hampshire and get Washington working for Granite Staters, not corporate special interests.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd978/2a380d31/16a49a3d/fd3ba9/2309204348/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYxMDAzX01XSF9uaC1uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20161003_MWH_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
I also wanted to say thank you -- your support has encouraged and inspired me. We're almost at the finish line, so please don't stop now.

clean_body: Hello there, It's been almost a year since I got in this race to fight for Granite State families in the U.S. Senate. It's hard to believe, but now there's just over a month left until this election is over. In the last year we've seen Senator Kelly Ayotte's corporate special interest backers throw everything they can at us, like $47 million in misleading ads. They know I will always put our families before their agenda, and frankly, they're scared. They also know that the Senate majority is on the line and that our race is their greatest chance at holding on to it. We're fighting back as hard as we can, but we need you to join us. "" Virtually every poll we've seen has had us within the margin of error, and the pundits are calling this race a toss-up. That means that this race is going to be decided by which team can work harder and turn out more people. Simply put, you can help determine this race. Every dollar you give will help us reach voters and turn out your neighbors. I've talked with our team, and they think you might be able to give $3 today. "" I also wanted to say thank you -- your support has encouraged and inspired me. We're almost at the finish line, so please don't stop now. With every good wish, Maggie Hassan ""

name: John Carney
subject: Educating our kids
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends for John Carney
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-5639543431198932992/-3696304105396895744?midqs=-4039447390774624256)
---
Friend, Education is essential for the success of our children and to our state as a whole. We've made some progress, but there is still so much more to be done. As governor, creating a quality education system for all Delaware children will be one of my top priorities. To do that, I'll focus on three key areas:
* **Ensuring that all children are getting to kindergarten ready to learn.** Our educators, community leaders, K-12 schools, students & their families, and our state government must collaborate to create quality early childhood education for all children in Delaware, which will prepare them for their educational journey.
* **Giving educators the tools and students the resources they need.** Because both of my parents were educators, I recognize that teachers are the most influential people in a student's education. We will need to refocus our Department of Education to more of a support system rather than a regulatory agency, so schools can better meet the individual needs of their students.
* **Making sure all students graduate high school career and college ready.** The bar set for students is higher than it's ever been before. We must rise to the challenge to prepare all Delaware students for this rapidly changing economy and create a strong workforce.

I understand how critical it is that we have an education system that provides opportunities for success for all children in Delaware. [**Click here to read my education plan, and learn how we will accomplish this goal.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-5639543431198932992/-3624246511358967808) Thanks for all your support, John | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/-5639543431198932992/-3696304105396895744?midqs=-4039447390774624256)[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-5639543431198932992/-3696304105396895744?midqs=-4039447390774624256)[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-5639543431198932992/-3696304105396895744?midqs=-4039447390774624256)

| **For Immediate Release:**
Monday, October 3, 2016

**Media Contact** : Ethan Latour
(802) 595-3776
**Phil Scott: Minter’s Higher Costs on Transportation Wrong for Economy, Wrong for Vermonters**
Montpelier, Vt. – Phil Scott today said his opponent’s so-called “cap and trade” plan for transportation would add unnecessary costs onto Vermont drivers and businesses, slow the economy and deepen the crisis of affordability facing many families and employers.

The [Burlington Free Press recently reported](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ff812a275e&e=19cebfe173) that a “cap and trade” proposal for gas and diesel in California has added at least 11-cents to the cost of a gallon of gas. Sue Minter has proposed replicating a version of that costly program in Vermont.

Scott said a ‘cap and trade’ regime is another way to add costs to transportation and will increase the costs of living across the board. “My opponent’s idea for a so-called ‘cap and trade’ system on gasoline and diesel fuel would drive up costs of fuel and the costs of virtually every commodity and service. It’s also another reminder that her approach to the challenges facing Vermont almost always involves increasing taxes and fees,” Scott said. “The choice in this campaign is clear: I want to grow the economy and make Vermont more affordable so we can afford to invest in priorities like early education and job training. Sue Minter believes making Vermonters pay more at the pump, increasing the cost of working, living, and doing business here is a good idea – I disagree.”

Scott also noted that the same Burlington Free Press story highlighting Minter’s so-called cap and trade gas and diesel tax proposal also pointed out that Minter has pledged to ‘limit the tax burden’ on middle income families.

“Limiting the tax burden on working families isn’t enough, especially when the cost of living are rising faster than wages,” Scott said. “I’m the only candidate in this race who knows that we need to be working to reduce the tax burden. To do that, we have to keep the state budget from growing faster than the economy and wages; stop imposing new taxes and fees; modernize state government; and make growing the economy the highest priority. If we don’t come to grips with this reality, we’ll continue to face higher taxes, a shrinking labor force and fewer resources to invest in programs we all support like early education, job training and substance abuse prevention and recovery.” ### **Paid for by Phil Scott For Vermont**
